<br/><br/>

<table>
<tr>
<td width="100%">

I don&#8217;t start with a spec. I start with a question &mdash; *what happens if I build this?*
That question has turned into a small universe of Telegram bots, apps, and tools shipped under
one name: **SHAZBOTZ**.

Most days that means deep, unglamorous systems work: pipelines that don&#8217;t break, filters
that scale, admin panels that make complexity disappear for the people using them. Some days it
means chasing an idea with no clear commercial reason to exist, purely because it&#8217;s
interesting.

I care about **architecture that survives growth** &mdash; one delivery system, one source of
truth, no duplicated logic bolted on as an afterthought. If a feature can&#8217;t feed into the
existing pipeline cleanly, it&#8217;s not done yet.

</td>
</tr>
</table>

<br/>
